summary: Recent updates in the testing framework for Firefox have improved the way graphics rendering is evaluated. By adjusting fuzz testing for Skia, a graphics library used for rendering 2D graphics, the browser can better identify subtle rendering issues. Additionally, the changes to bump the Android fuzz boundary and streamline macOS version annotations for color_quads reftests enhance compatibility and efficiency in testing across different platforms. Moreover, utilizing quad shaders for images when **TEXTURE_RECT isn't required** boosts performance by optimizing how images are processed during testing, ensuring a smoother visual experience.
